steps.title: Library structure U6 stub-[19-20-nt gRNA]-gRNA hairpin-[PBS/RT]-tevO-Q1...
steps.instruction: Library structure U6 stub-[19-20-nt gRNA]-gRNA hairpin-[PBS/RT]-tevO-Q1 motif-terminator-[Reporter]-[7 nt barcode]-R2seq stub tgtggaaaggacgaaacaccg[19-nt gRNA spacer] GTTTCAGAGCTATGCTGGAAACAGCATAGCAAGTTGAAATAAGGCTAGTCCGTTATCAACTTGAAAAAGTGGCACCGAGTCGGTGC[PBS/RT]CGCGGTTCTATCTAGTTACGCGTTAAACCAACTAGAATTTTTT[Reporter][7-nt barcode]AGATCGGAAGAGCACACGTCT

steps.title: Reconstitution of ssDNA oligo pool
steps.instruction: Reconstitution of ssDNA oligo pool

steps.title: Spin down lyophilized ssDNA oligo pool
steps.instruction: Spin down lyophilized ssDNA oligo pool

steps.title: Prepare 2 ng/µL stock by resuspending...
steps.instruction: Prepare 2 ng/µL stock by resuspending in TE buffer with low EDTA (10 mM Tris-Cl pH 8.0, 0.1 mM EDTA)

steps.title: Store at -20°C
steps.instruction: Store at -20°C.

steps.title: Restriction digest of pLenti U6_r2ad_IN-PE2-SSB_PuroR library backbone
steps.instruction: Restriction digest of pLenti U6_r2ad_IN-PE2-SSB_PuroR library backbone

steps.title: Cut 10 µg pLenti U6_r2ad_IN-PE2-SSB_PuroR with BsmBI-V2
steps.instruction: Cut 10 µg pLenti U6_r2ad_IN-PE2-SSB_PuroR with BsmBI-V2

steps.title: Restriction digest reaction mix overview
steps.instruction: Restriction digest reaction mix overview: A B 1 H2O to 100 µL 2 NEBuffer r3.1 10 µL 3 pLenti U6_r2ad_ IN-PE2-SSB_PuroR 10 µg 4 BsmBI-v2 10 units

steps.title: Incubate at 55 °C for 3-4 hours with BsmBI-V2
steps.instruction: Incubate at 55 °C for 3-4 hours with BsmBI-V2

steps.title: Run digest on 1% agarose gel...
steps.instruction: Run digest on 1% agarose gel with SYBR and cut out band at 16.38 kbp. Distribute 100 uL digest reaction among 3 large agarose gel wells (12 tooth comb, 1.5 mm thickness).

steps.title: Amplification of library Amplification of library...
steps.instruction: Amplification of library Amplification of library done with the following primers: 010415_sgRNA_60bp_fw TAACTTGAAAGTATTTCGATTTCTTGGCTTTATATATCT TGTGGAAAGGACGAAACACCG 062024_r2seq_i722_BsrGrv TTTAAAACTTTATCCATCTTTGCATGTACA GAAGACGGCATACGAGAT NN CTGCGC GTGACTGGAGTTC AGACGTGTGCTCTTCCGATCT

steps.title: Determination of optimal PCR cycles for...
steps.instruction: Determination of optimal PCR cycles for library amplification Run a qPCR with 0.5 µL of 100924_fullLDLR_pegRNA_library in 15 µL qPCR to determine optimal cycles using the primers above. qPCR mix %s %s qPCR program A B C D E F 1 98 °C 98 °C 69 °C 72 °C 72 °C 4 °C 2 30 sec 10 sec 30 sec 60 sec 5 min remaining Repeat steps B-D for 25 cycles Identify the cycle number where the qPCR stops log-linear increase (this is typically 4-6 cycles after the CT), determine how much more input in PCR as in qPCR and subtract 5 cycles to identify PCR cycle number.

steps.title: Gibson assembly %s %s Incubate at 50°C for 1 hour
steps.instruction: Gibson assembly %s %s Incubate at 50°C for 1 hour.

steps.title: Cleanup/concentration
steps.instruction: Cleanup/concentration

steps.title: Add 1 µL GlycoBlue, 2 µL...
steps.instruction: Add 1 µL GlycoBlue, 2 µL 50mM NaCl, and 100 µL Isopropanol to the 100 µL Gibson reaction

steps.title: Vortex, incubate at room temperature for 15 min
steps.instruction: Vortex, incubate at room temperature for 15 min

steps.title: Spin > 15,000 x g for 15 min
steps.instruction: Spin > 15,000 x g for 15 min

steps.title: Carefully remove liquid without disturbing pellet
steps.instruction: Carefully remove liquid without disturbing pellet. This can be achieved by using a P200 and a P20/P10 afterwards to remove all liquid.

steps.title: Wash with 300 µL 80% EtOH...
steps.instruction: Wash with 300 µL 80% EtOH and spin at >15,000 x g for 5 min

steps.title: Remove most liquid with P1000 and...
steps.instruction: Remove most liquid with P1000 and spin at >15,000 x g for 1 min

steps.title: Carefully remove all liquid with P200,...
steps.instruction: Carefully remove all liquid with P200, making sure tube has no liquid left (this can be achieved by using a P200 and a P20/P10 afterwards to remove all liquid) and air-dry pellet for 3-5 minutes by keeping cap open and leaving at room temperature

steps.title: Add 8
steps.instruction: Add 8.25 µL EB, warming at 55°C for 10 minutes to fully resuspend

steps.title: Determination of Coverage and Diversity of...
steps.instruction: Determination of Coverage and Diversity of Cloned Library Transformation of a small amount of the assembly mix for the determination of diversity and coverage of the cloned library.

steps.title: Add 2 µL of assembly mix...
steps.instruction: Add 2 µL of assembly mix to 25 uL of Lucigen Endura electrocompetent cells, repeat 4x

steps.title: Electroporate using the following parameters
steps.instruction: Electroporate using the following parameters: 1mm cuvette (pre-chilled on ice) 10 μF 600 Ohms 1800 Volts

steps.title: Immediately add 1 mL recovery media...
steps.instruction: Immediately add 1 mL recovery media from Lucigen into the electroporation cuvette.

steps.title: Gently pipet bacterial suspension into a...
steps.instruction: Gently pipet bacterial suspension into a 14 mL culture tube that already contains 1 mL of Lucigen recovery media

steps.title: Incubate at 30°C for 1 hour while shaking
steps.instruction: Incubate at 30°C for 1 hour while shaking.

steps.title: Production and Titration of Lentivirus
steps.instruction: Production and Titration of Lentivirus

steps.title: Determination of CT Values for Pooling...
steps.instruction: Determination of CT Values for Pooling in PCR1 Perform qPCR1 to determine CT values of each sample. These values will be used to pool samples prior to PCR1 purification. Use the following primers: 101317_U6PE1_BcX (ACTCTTTCCCTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCT NNNNN GGAAAGGACGAAACACCG) 112822_P7_anchor (CAAG CAGAAGACGGCATACGAGAT)

steps.title: PCR1 for cell-integrated libraries Choose a...
steps.instruction: PCR1 for cell-integrated libraries Choose a distinct U6PE1 barcoded primer for each sample to the extent possible (we have 24 distinct primers). This helps to minimize monotemplate because the barcoded primers are staggered, and it allowsdemultiplexing of each sample with a distinct U6PE1 during NGS analysis. You may also pool samples during PCR1 purification if they have distinct U6PE1s and qPCR1 showed Ct <0.5 cycles apart.

steps.title: %s %s Cycling (25 cycles)
steps.instruction: %s %s Cycling (25 cycles): %s %s

steps.title: You may combine samples with similar CTs (typically within 0
steps.instruction: You may combine samples with similar CTs (typically within 0.5 cycles, pool bulk samples separately from sorted samples) provided that they used distinct U6PE1 primers in PCR1. Add 5x volume of buffer PB and proceed to PCR purify via Qiagen PCR purification kit each pool using 1 column per pool, eluting in 100 uL EB per pool. Note that you may want to use tube extenders since there can be a lot of volume to go through one column.

steps.title: Determination of CT Values for Optimal Cycle Number in PCR2
steps.instruction: Determination of CT Values for Optimal Cycle Number in PCR2

steps.title: PCR2
steps.instruction: PCR2

steps.title: PCR 2 50 uL Reaction system
steps.instruction: PCR 2 50 uL Reaction system: %s %s %s %s Amplified Library sequence: ~340-400bp AATGATACGGCGACCACCGAGATCTACACTCTTTCCCTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCT NNNNNggaaaggacgaaacaccg GGCTGCGAGCATGGGGCCCGTTTCAGAGCTATGCTGGAAACAGCATAGCAAGTTGAAATAAGGCTAGTCCGTTATCAACTTGAAAAAGTGGCACCGAGTCGGTGCGCAATTTCCAGCCggcGGGCCCCATGCTCGCAGCCGCGGTTCTATCTAGTTACGCGTTAAACCAACTAGAATTTTTTGAGGCTGCGAGCATGGGGCCCTGGGGCTGGAAATTGCTTGAGCG AGATCGGAAGAGCACACGTCTGAACTCCAGTCACNNNNNNNNATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG

steps.title: PCR purify PCR2 onto 1 column each
steps.instruction: PCR purify PCR2 onto 1 column each. Elute in 50 µl of EB buffer.

steps.title: Library amplification Once optimal cycle number...
steps.instruction: Library amplification Once optimal cycle number is determined, run a 100 µL PCR with the following conditions: %s %s %s %s Load PCR onto a 2% agarose gel with SYBR (3 large wells, 12 tooth comb, 1.5 mm thickness) and gel purify band around 350-410 bp. There will be a smear in this size range: make to include the entire size range, as pegRNA length varies and we want to include all pegRNAs. Elute in 50 µL EB. Perform PCR purification on this eluted gel purification. Start by mixing 50 µL eluted gel purification product with 250 µL buffer PB and continue with standard Qiagen PCR purification protocol. Elute in 30 µL EB.

steps.title: Add 0
steps.instruction: Add 0.25 µL of the purified assembled mix into a 1.5 mL tube and gently mix with 25 µL of NEB Stable cells. Incubate for 30 minutes on ice.

steps.title: Electroporation of Assembly Mix in Endura Cells
steps.instruction: Electroporation of Assembly Mix in Endura Cells

steps.title: Heat shock at exactly 42°C for...
steps.instruction: Heat shock at exactly 42°C for 30 seconds and put back on ice for 5 minutes.

steps.title: Add 975 µL of NEB 10-beta/Stable...
steps.instruction: Add 975 µL of NEB 10-beta/Stable Outgrowth Medium and incubate at 30°C for 1 hour while shaking.

steps.title: Plate 1/10, 1/100, and 1/1,000 of...
steps.instruction: Plate 1/10, 1/100, and 1/1,000 of transformation mix onto LB agar plates (supplied with 100 µg/mL ampicillin). Incubate at 30°C overnight.

steps.title: Calculation of the Coverage Choose the...
steps.instruction: Calculation of the Coverage Choose the dilution plate with colony numbers in the range between 20-200 and count the colonies. Calculate the coverage as follows: ((Counted colonies * dilution factor) / Number of library members) * 1600 A coverage of >100x is ideal. Note: 0.25 µL of the remaining 8 µL purified assembly mix is 32x used in this test. Electroporation with Endura typically yields in a 50x higher transformation efficiency compared to NEB Stable cells, meaning that the entire remaining 8 µL of the assembly mix has 32x50=1600x the number of colonies as the test transformation with 0.25 µL.

steps.title: Purify rest of the colony PCR...
steps.instruction: Purify rest of the colony PCR via QIAquick PCR Purification Kit and send in for Sanger sequencing with primer 091324_U6_latefw (ATTTCTTGGGTAGTTTGCAGTTT). Check the cloned sgRNAs for diversity and move on to the electroporation of the assembly mix into Endura cells.

steps.title: Determination of the Diversity Perform colony...
steps.instruction: Determination of the Diversity Perform colony PCR on 16 colonies from any of the dilution plates to determined the diversity of the cloned library, using the following conditions: 091324_U6_latefw ATTTCTTGGGTAGTTTGCAGTTT 031621_postSmarv GCTGCAAAGATTCCTCTCTG PCR mix for one reaction: %s %s PCR program: %s %s

steps.title: Load 5 µL of each PCR...
steps.instruction: Load 5 µL of each PCR onto a 2% agarose gel with ethidium bromide and expect bands at 400-500 bp for successful cloning, while >1000 bp indicates plasmid background. If >2 colonies have 1000 bp bands, then re-clone.

steps.title: In a 50 mL centrifugation tube,...
steps.instruction: In a 50 mL centrifugation tube, pool all 4 electroporation mixes together and mix well by swirling.

steps.title: Take out 10 µL of the...
steps.instruction: Take out 10 µL of the pooled electroporation mix and add to 1 mL of recovery medium. Plate out 20 and 100 µL of that dilution onto pre-warmed plates (supplied with 100 µg/mL ampicillin, 40,000 and 8,000-fold dilution, respectively). Incubate at 30°C overnight and count colonies to determine the coverage (see step 7.5 for calculation).

steps.title: Transfer the pooled electroporation mix into...
steps.instruction: Transfer the pooled electroporation mix into 400 mL LB medium supplied with 100 µg/mL ampicillin. Incubate at 30°C overnight while shaking.

steps.title: Centrifuge bacterial suspension as 2x200 mL...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge bacterial suspension as 2x200 mL aliquots (3000 x g, 4°C, 20 minutes). Maxi prep one bacterial pellet and keep the other one as backup at -20°C.

steps.title: Day -1
steps.instruction: Day -1: Plate HEK293 cells for Transfection Plate 4x15 cm plates with HEK293FT cells at 1.625 x 10 7 cells per 15-cm plate in 20 mL DMEM + 10% FBS each

steps.title: Day 0
steps.instruction: Day 0: Transfection of Lentiviral Plasmid Library Cells should be 50-60% confluent. Aspirate media and replace with 16 mL DMEM + FBS (one plate at a time since HEK cells do not tolerate being dry). Prepare 2 separate tubes with the following components per 15-cm plate: Tube A A B 1 OptiMEM 4 mL 2 pMDLg/pRRE 9.7 µg 3 pRSV-Rev 6.5 µg 4 pcDNA3-VSV-G 3.3 µg 5 Lentiviral Plasmid Library 13 µg Tube B A B 1 OptiMEM 4 mL 2 TransIT-Lenti Transfection Reagent 98 µl Mix the contents of tube B into A and gently invert. Incubate for 15 minutes at room temperature.

steps.title: Gently add transfection mix dropwise to the cells
steps.instruction: Gently add transfection mix dropwise to the cells.

steps.title: Day 1
steps.instruction: Day 1: First Harvest Collect lentiviral supernatant and store at 4°C. Replace medium with 16 mL of DMEM+10% FBS.

steps.title: Day 2
steps.instruction: Day 2: Second Harvest Collect lentiviral supernatant and store at 4°C. Replace medium with 16 mL of DMEM+10% FBS.

steps.title: Day 3
steps.instruction: Day 3: Final Harvest and Lentivirus Concetration Collect lentiviral supernatant and pool all lentivirus harvest together.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 1,500 x g for 45 minutes at 4°C
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 1,500 x g for 45 minutes at 4°C.

steps.title: Gently pour out the supernatant and...
steps.instruction: Gently pour out the supernatant and resuspend pellet in 2 mL of DMEM+10% FBS per 15 cm. Aliquot 500 µL per cryovial and store at -80°C, saving two 60 µL aliquots for lentiviral titration

steps.title: Centrifuge pooled lentiviral supernatant at 300...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge pooled lentiviral supernatant at 300 x g for 5 minutes. Filter supernatant through a 0.45 µm filter and add 1/3 supernatant volume of Lenti-X Concentrator. Mix gently by inversion and incubate for 30 minutes or overnight at 4°C.

steps.title: Titration of Lentivirus Titrate the lentivirus...
steps.instruction: Titration of Lentivirus Titrate the lentivirus on a 24 well plate using 5.2 x 10 4 /well (2.6 x 10 4 /cm2). Eventual experiment will use 3.91M (3.91 x 10 6 ) cells on a 15-cm (2.6 x 10 4 /cm2). Each 24-well of titration uses ~1/75 as many cells as the 15-cm plate will. Below is the standard lentivirus titration dose chart, although you can alter if necessary. Volumes are calculated for 4 mL total lenti and should be adjusted as necessary to account for fraction of lenti produced: ‱ 1/150 = 26.66 µL ‱ 1/300 = 13.33 µL ‱ 1/600 = 6.66 µL ‱ 1/1200 = 3.33 µL ‱ 1/1800 = 2.22 µL ‱ 1/2400 = 1.67 µL ‱ 1/4800 = 0.83 µL

steps.title: Day 0
steps.instruction: Day 0: Plating and Infection of HCT116 Cells Plate 52,000 HCT116 cells per well in a 24-well supplied with polybrene in a final concentration of 8 µg/mL. Add lentivirus in the dilution steps as stated above. Have two extra wells seeded with no virus as selection control.

steps.title: Day 2
steps.instruction: Day 2: Start of Puromycin Selection Replace medium with DMEM+10% FBS supplied with 500 ng/mL puromycin

steps.title: Day 3
steps.instruction: Day 3: First Passage Wash cells with PBS and add 75 µL of trypsin. Incubate for 5 minutes at 37°C and add 500 µL of medium supplied with puromycin. Mix detached cells with a P1000 and transfer cell suspension to a new 24-well.

steps.title: Day 5
steps.instruction: Day 5: Second Passage Wash cells with PBS and add 75 µL of trypsin. Incubate for 5 minutes at 37°C and add 500 µL of medium supplied with puromycin. Mix detached cells with a P1000 and transfer cell suspension to a new 24-well.

steps.title: Day 7
steps.instruction: Day 7: Count By now, control well with no virus should be completely dead. Count each well and detemine the lentiviral dose with the highest survival. The lentiviral dose with 50% survival from that is then the desired lentiviral dosage for the screen. Multiply the dosage with 75 to scale the lentiviral dosage to the 15 cm dish format.

steps.title: Lentivirus Library Infection and LDL
steps.instruction: Lentivirus Library Infection and LDL

steps.title: Day 0
steps.instruction: Day 0: Infection of HCT116 Cells Trypsinze and count HCT cells. Add 3.91 x 10 6 cells to a 15 mL centrifugation tube, add appropriate amount of lentivirus library as calculated from the titration and mix by gently inverting. Plate mix onto 15 cm dish with a total medium volume of 20 ml supplied with 8 µg/mL polybrene. Repeat this for a total number of 4 replicates. Seed 266,000 HCT116 cells in a 6-well as selection control.

steps.title: Day 3
steps.instruction: Day 3: Start of Puromycin Selection Replace medium with 20 µL of McCoy+10% FBS supplied with 500 ng/mL puromycin.

steps.title: Day 4
steps.instruction: Day 4: First Passage Split cells 1:2 to one new 15 cm dish with 20 ml of McCoy+10% FBS supplied with 500 ng/mL puromycin (ending up with one plate per replicate).

steps.title: Day 6
steps.instruction: Day 6: Second Passage Split cells 1:2 to two new 15 cm dish with 20 mL of McCoy+10% FBS supplied with 500 ng/ml puromycin (ending up with two plates per replicate). By now, control 6-well with no virus should be dead.

steps.title: Day 8
steps.instruction: Day 8: Seed for LDL Uptake Screen Seed 31.25 x 10 6 cells per replicate in a 15 cm dish. For bulk, seed 12.25 x 10 6 cells in a 10 cm dish. Seed both in McCoy+10% FBS.

steps.title: Day 9
steps.instruction: Day 9: Serum Starvation In the late afternoon, change medium to OptiMEM to start serum starvation.

steps.title: qPCR for one reaction
steps.instruction: qPCR for one reaction: A B 1 gDNA 0.5 µL 2 2x Q5 Ultra II Master Mix 7.5 µL 3 101317_U6PE1_BcX (20 µM) 0.375 µL 4 112822_P7_anchor (20 µM) 0.375 µL 5 20x EvaGreen 0.75 µL 6 dH20 5.5 µL qPCR1 program %s %s After collecting CT values for each sample, organize samples into pools such that samples in each pool have CT values within 0.5 cycles of each other. This step is done to minimize the complexity and time of PCR1 purification and further steps of NGS preparation (qPCR2, PCR2). Note that bulk gDNA samples should never be pooled with sorted samples, regardless of CT similarity. Also note that everysample you wish to pool after PCR1 MUST use a distinct U6PE1 barcoded primer.

steps.title: %s %s %s %s
steps.instruction: %s %s %s %s

steps.title: Use 2-3 cycles less than the...
steps.instruction: Use 2-3 cycles less than the qPCR Ct for PCR2 (it’s OK to use up to 3 cycles less than qPCR Ct and up to qPCR Ct so as to group samples most efficiently). Minimum number of PCR2 cycles is 5. If 2-3 below the CT value is <5, then use less PCR1 product input for PCR2 to compensate for 5 cycles. Ex: if your CT is 5, then 2 cycles below the CT would be 3. In order to run the minimum of 5 cycles, add in 1/4 of the normal amount of PCR1 product (so 6ul) to compensate for the two extra PCR cycles.

steps.title: Tapestation Use D1000 tapescreen only, make...
steps.instruction: Tapestation Use D1000 tapescreen only, make sure to use D1000 ladder and buffer. In PCR tubes: Add 3 uL of buffer + either 1 uL of ladder or sample Pooling sampling based on nM from Tapestation into a single tube Pooling Calculation: [(desired read per sample)/(desired total read across all samples)]/Tapestation nM*X000(Random number to get a reasonably pipettable amount)

steps.title: SPRI bead Purification
steps.instruction: SPRI bead Purification : to remove any undesirable bands (eg primer dimers) observedon gel. Make a pool of sample, mix well, take ½ for SPRI bead purification. Add dH2O or EB upto 50 uL. Perform 0.80X bead purification (add 40 uL beads to pool) because product is ~340-400 bp. Elute from beads with 20 µl EB buffer.

steps.title: Day 10
steps.instruction: Day 10: LDL Uptake Screen 1. 4-6 hours prior to the FACS sort, replace medium of each replicate plate with 16 mL ofOptiMEM supplied 40 µL with Low Density Lipoprotein from Human Plasma, BODIPYFL complex (BODIPY FL LDL).2. Harvest one plate at the time - trypsinze and spin down to collect cell pellet,resuspend in DMEM+10% FBS supplied with DAPI3. Sort cells based on BODIPY signal in the following bins: Bottom 20%, Bottom 20-40%, Top 20-40%, and Top 20%.4. Isolate genomic DNA from sorted cells and bulk cells via PureLink Genomic DNA Mini Kit and elute gDNA in 100 µL.

steps.title: Collect >400 average reads per library...
steps.instruction: Collect >400 average reads per library member to be safe (so for 6,000-member library, aim for >2.5M reads). Read 1: 150 nt Read 2: 150 nt Index 1: 8 nt Index 2: 8 nt
